message:
feat(fnp-python): add hfft/rfft2/irfft2 passthrough wrappers (br-franken_numpy-iq7n, br-franken_numpy-4ln8, br-franken_numpy-uxm5)

Expose three missing FFT wrappers delegating to numpy.fft:

- hfft(a, n=None, axis=-1, norm=None, out=None) — FFT of a
  Hermitian-symmetric signal, returning a real spectrum. Full numpy
  kwarg surface forwarded.

- rfft2(a, s=None, axes=None, norm=None, out=None) — 2-D real-input
  FFT. axes default is (-2, -1) in numpy. Output last-axis length is
  n//2+1; output dtype is complex.

- irfft2(a, s=None, axes=None, norm=None, out=None) — 2-D inverse
  real FFT. Output dtype is real (float64).

Passthrough style matches existing fft2/ifft2/irfft wrappers: signatures
mirror numpy, kwargs forwarded via PyDict, call through
numpy.fft.<name>.

Joint parity oracle (`hfft_rfft2_irfft2_match_numpy_across_shapes_axes_norms_and_roundtrip`):
- hfft:   1-D Hermitian default; explicit n=8 norm='ortho';
          real dtype verification; hfft(ihfft(x)) ≈ x round-trip.
- rfft2:  4x4 arange default; output shape (4, 3) verification
          (last axis = n//2+1 for n=4); axes=(0,1) norm='ortho'.
- irfft2: default parity with ours_r from rfft2; irfft2(rfft2(x)) ≈ x
          round-trip for even shape; real dtype verification;
          s=(4,4) norm='forward' round-trip.

Verified via rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-python --lib
hfft_rfft2_irfft2: 1 passed, 0 failed; finished in 0.29s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
